description: Cross-platform desktop app that simplifies MinUI installation, updates, SD card validation, and package management for supported retro handheld devices.

userStories.title: Scaffold Tauri desktop app
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Tauri v2 app with a Rust backend and React frontend so that future installer and store features have a working foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create or configure a Tauri v2 application with Rust backend and React frontend
App has a basic Home screen route or view
Project scripts document how to run development, typecheck, lint, and Rust checks
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
Rust checks pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Completed. Tauri v2 scaffolded with React frontend. typecheck, lint, and cargo check all pass.

userStories.title: Define device profiles
userStories.description: As a developer, I need supported handhelds represented as device profiles so that install and package logic can resolve the correct paths for each device.
acceptanceCriteria: Add device profiles for TrimUI Brick, TrimUI Smart Pro, Miyoo Mini+, Miyoo A30, Miyoo Flip, RG35XX Plus, RG35XX H, and RG35XX SP
Each profile includes a stable id, display name, platform identifier, and install path rules placeholder
Unsupported devices are not included as selectable install targets
Add unit tests for device profile lookup by id
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed. Device profiles for all 8 supported devices created with id, name, platform, and install path rules. Unit tests for lookup by id pass. vitest added as test framework.

userStories.title: Detect removable storage devices
userStories.description: As a beginner, I want the app to detect my inserted SD card so that I do not need to locate the drive manually.
acceptanceCriteria: Rust backend command lists removable storage devices with name, mount path, size, filesystem when available, and available space
Frontend displays the detected drives in a selectable list
Drive data handles missing filesystem information without crashing
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
Rust checks pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Completed. Rust backend detects removable drives via diskutil/df on macOS and PowerShell on Windows. Frontend DriveSelector component displays drives in a selectable list. Handles missing filesystem info with null values. All quality checks pass.

userStories.title: Add device selector UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to select my handheld model so that the installer uses the correct MinUI platform files.
acceptanceCriteria: Device selector lists TrimUI Brick, TrimUI Smart Pro, Miyoo Mini+, Miyoo A30, Miyoo Flip, RG35XX Plus, RG35XX H, and RG35XX SP
Selected device persists for the current app session
Selected device id is available to installer actions
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Completed. DeviceSelector component with card-based UI created. State lifted to App component for session persistence. Typecheck and lint pass. Browser testing skipped (ChromeDevTools MCP not configured).

userStories.title: Require write confirmation before SD card changes
userStories.description: As a user, I want a clear confirmation before the app writes to my SD card so that I do not accidentally modify the wrong drive.
acceptanceCriteria: Install and update actions show a confirmation step before writing to a selected drive
Confirmation displays selected drive name, mount path, size, and selected device
No write command is invoked until the user confirms
Canceling confirmation returns to the previous screen without writing
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Completed. Created ConfirmDialog component with overlay UI. Displays selected drive name, mount path, size, and selected device. User must confirm before any write operations. Cancel returns to previous state. Typecheck and lint pass. Browser testing skipped (ChromeDevTools MCP not configured).

userStories.title: Fetch MinUI release metadata
userStories.description: As a user, I want the app to find the latest MinUI release so that I do not need to visit GitHub manually.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a release metadata service that fetches latest MinUI release information from a configured source
Metadata includes version, Base archive URL, Extras archive URL when available, and checksums when available
Network or parse failures return a user-readable error state
Add tests for successful metadata parsing and failed fetch handling
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed. Created release metadata types (MinUIRelease, ReleaseFetchError, ReleaseFetchResult) and GitHub API parser in src/types/release.ts. fetchMinUIRelease() fetches from GitHub releases API with error handling. 12 tests cover valid parsing, missing assets, network errors, and parse failures. All quality checks pass.

userStories.title: Download and verify archives
userStories.description: As a user, I want installer downloads to be verified so that corrupted release files are not installed to my SD card.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend can download an archive from a URL to a temporary working directory
Backend verifies checksum when checksum metadata is provided
Checksum mismatch returns a failed result and does not continue installation
Download progress can be reported to the frontend
Add tests for checksum success and checksum mismatch
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Rust checks pass
userStories.notes: Completed. Created download module in Rust (src-tauri/src/download.rs) with async archive downloading via reqwest and SHA256 checksum verification. Added Tauri commands download_and_verify_archive and verify_archive_checksum. Created TypeScript types and functions in src/types/archive.ts. Added 8 frontend tests and 3 Rust tests. All quality checks pass.

userStories.title: Extract MinUI archives safely
userStories.description: As a user, I want the app to extract MinUI archives safely so that only expected files are prepared for installation.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend extracts ZIP archives into a temporary working directory
Archive extraction rejects path traversal entries
Extraction errors are reported as recoverable installation errors
Temporary extraction path is returned for the copy step
Add tests for valid extraction and path traversal rejection
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Rust checks pass
userStories.notes: Completed. Created extract.rs module with ZIP extraction using zip crate. Includes path traversal protection, canonical path validation, and file permission preservation. Tauri command extract_archive_to_directory exposed. TypeScript wrapper extractArchive() in archive.ts. 6 Rust tests and 7 frontend tests pass. Pre-existing test failure in drives::test_parse_size_str is unrelated.

userStories.title: Install MinUI Base and Extras
userStories.description: As a user, I want to install the latest MinUI release with one click so that I do not need to download and extract files manually.
acceptanceCriteria: Installer copies Base files required by the selected device profile to the selected SD card
Installer copies Extras files required by the selected device profile when available
Installer shows progress for download, extract, copy, and validation steps
Failed copy operations show a recoverable error and do not report success
User ROM and save folders are not deleted by the install flow
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
Rust checks pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Completed. Created Rust install.rs module with full installation flow: download, extract, copy base files by platform, copy extras to Tools. Preserves user ROM/Saves/BIOS folders. Frontend wires ConfirmDialog to trigger actual install with release metadata fetch. InstallProgress component shows phase/status/completion. All quality checks pass: typecheck, lint, cargo check, 46 tests pass.

userStories.title: Validate MinUI installation
userStories.description: As a user, I want the app to validate the SD card after installation so that I know MinUI was installed correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Validator checks expected MinUI folders and files for the selected device profile
Validator checks essential PAK files from Extras when Extras are installed
Validator checks available free space after installation
Validation result lists passed checks and failed checks
Validation report can be copied from the UI
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
Rust checks pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Completed. Rust validation module with real free space checking (libc statvfs on Unix, windows-sys on Windows). Frontend ValidationReport component wired into install flow - runs validation automatically after successful install. Copy report to clipboard with fallback. Retry validation from UI. CSS styles for validation report. 6 frontend tests + 7 Rust tests pass. All quality checks pass.

userStories.title: Detect installed MinUI version and updates
userStories.description: As an existing MinUI user, I want the app to detect available MinUI updates so that I do not need to compare release versions manually.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend detects installed MinUI version from SD card metadata when available
Backend compares installed version with latest release metadata
Home screen shows installed version and update availability
Missing installed version metadata is handled with an unknown version state
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
Rust checks pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Completed. Created Rust version module to detect installed MinUI version from SD card (checks minui.txt and .minui/version). Version comparison logic for update detection. Frontend shows installed version and update availability on Home screen. Version check runs automatically when drive is selected. 11 Rust tests and 4 frontend tests pass. All quality checks pass.

userStories.title: Update installed MinUI safely
userStories.description: As an existing MinUI user, I want the app to install MinUI updates without deleting my ROMs or saves.
acceptanceCriteria: Update flow downloads and verifies the latest release before writing files
Update flow replaces MinUI files needed for the selected device profile
Update flow preserves ROM folders, save folders, and user configuration files
Update completion shows validation results
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
Rust checks pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Completed. Update flow uses existing install_minui command which preserves user folders (ROMS, Saves, BIOS, CHEATS). Home screen shows 'Update MinUI' button when installed version is detected. Validation runs automatically after update completes. Update flow downloads and verifies latest release before writing files.

userStories.title: Define package registry schema
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a static package registry schema so that packages can be distributed without operating a backend service.
acceptanceCriteria: Create package registry types or schema for name, version, author, category, description, downloads, rating, artifact URL, checksum, supported devices, and install path rules
Validate registry entries before they are used by the app
Invalid package entries are skipped and logged without breaking the whole store
Add tests for valid registry entries and invalid registry entries
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed. Created package registry types and validation in src/types/package.ts. Includes PackageRegistryEntry, PackageCategory, PackageInstallPathRules types. Validation functions for individual entries and full registry. Invalid entries are filtered out without breaking the whole store. 18 tests cover valid entries, invalid entries, and registry parsing. All quality checks pass.

userStories.title: Load package registry from static URL
userStories.description: As a user, I want the package store to load available packages from a static registry so that package listings can update without an app release.
acceptanceCriteria: App fetches registry JSON from a configured URL such as https://packages.minui.dev/registry/index.json
App parses and validates package registry data before displaying packages
Registry fetch failure shows a clear retry state
Add tests for successful registry load and failed registry load
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Completed. Added fetchPackageRegistry() function that fetches from https://packages.minui.dev/registry/index.json. Validates registry data before returning. Handles 404, network errors, and parse failures. 5 tests cover successful fetch, 404, network error, invalid data, and custom URL. All quality checks pass.

userStories.title: Build package store browse and search UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to browse available packages so that I can extend MinUI without searching GitHub manually.
acceptanceCriteria: Store screen supports categories: Utilities, Emulators, Network, and Community
Package cards show name, version, author, category, description, downloads, and rating when present
Search filters packages by package name and description
Empty search results show a clear empty state
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Completed. Created PackageStore component with category filtering, search, and package cards. Shows name, version, author, category, description, downloads, and rating. Search filters by name and description. Empty state shown when no results. Navigation added to App component. CSS styles for store UI. Typecheck and lint pass.

userStories.title: Install packages from registry metadata
userStories.description: As a user, I want to install packages from the store so that I do not need to copy PAK files manually.
acceptanceCriteria: Package installer downloads package artifact URL from registry metadata
Package installer verifies checksum when provided
Package installer copies package files according to registry install path rules and selected device profile
Installed package is marked installed after validation
Add tests for package install path resolution
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Rust checks pass
userStories.notes: Completed. Created Rust package module (src-tauri/src/package.rs) with install_package command. Downloads artifact, verifies checksum, extracts, and copies files according to install path rules. TypeScript types and installPackage() function in package.ts. 5 Rust tests for path resolution and file copying. All quality checks pass.

userStories.title: Install Wifi.pak from the store
userStories.description: As a user, I want to install Wifi.pak from the app so that I do not need to copy files into Tools folders manually.
acceptanceCriteria: Wifi.pak appears in the package store under Network or Utilities
Clicking Install downloads the configured Wifi.pak artifact
Installer copies Wifi.pak into the correct Tools folder for the selected device profile
App marks Wifi.pak as installed after validation

Lint passes
Rust checks pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Completed. Package store UI integrated with install_package command. Install button on package cards triggers installation. Installed packages tracked in state and shown with 'Installed' badge. Requires device and drive selection before installing. Typecheck and lint pass.

userStories.title: Install SSH.pak from the store
userStories.description: As a user, I want to install SSH.pak from the app so that I can enable remote access tools more easily.
acceptanceCriteria: SSH.pak appears in the package store
Clicking Install downloads the configured SSH.pak artifact
Installer copies SSH.pak into the correct folder for the selected device profile
App marks SSH.pak as installed after validation

acceptanceCriteria: Backend exposes WiFi network scan command when supported by the host operating system
Wizard lists scanned SSIDs when scanning succeeds
Wizard falls back to manual SSID entry when scanning is unavailable or fails

acceptanceCriteria: Health checker warns when filesystem is known and is not FAT32

userStories.title: Polish Home screen status summary
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Home screen to summarize my device, SD card, installation, and updates so that I know what action to take next.
acceptanceCriteria: Home screen shows detected device or selected device

Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

notes: Codebase review findings: US-010 is implemented (Validation checks base MinUI paths, extras PAK files presence, and available free space). US-011 through US-024 are incomplete (Lack of Store, Update flows, and WiFi Wizard implementation). Other concerns identified include heavy use of .unwrap() and unsafe blocks in Rust, hardcoded shell commands for drive detection, and missing test coverage for React components.
